page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel processor. It features 80 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration provides 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25% tier. Single-core performance remains in the average range. The instance excels in static web serving throughput and image processing, both ranking in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, registering strong read and write speeds even with large uncached blocks. Cryptographic operations and database tasks also perform in the top 25% tier.

Given its high compute-to-memory ratio and lack of local storage, the n4-highcpu-80 is designed for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe drives. It is highly suitable for high-throughput web serving, image processing, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference, particularly leveraging Intel AMX for matrix operations. The instance offers a cost-efficient profile for parallel processing tasks by maximizing CPU density while avoiding the added expense of bundled storage or GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs across 40 physical Intel cores and 160.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is tailored for raw CPU throughput, offering 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU and a 260 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel AMX and AVX-512. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in static web serving and image processing, alongside strong multi-core execution and memory bandwidth. The n4-highcpu-80 provides a cost-effective architecture for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads by omitting expensive storage and accelerator add-ons. It is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and advanced AMX support, delivering top-tier performance for web serving and image processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-80 features 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 160 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring eighty dedicated cores and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB RAM on Intel x86_64 architecture
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier performance in static web serving and image processing benchmarks
Strong multi-core processing and high memory bandwidth capabilities
No bundled local storage or GPUs, maximizing compute-to-cost efficiency
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based inference
